scope.summary: Implement a round-trip Handshake Garment Archive (.hgz) export and import that bundles GarmentSpec, material presets, UV sets, trim definitions, and animation cache into one portable file transferable between Handshake instances
acceptance_criteria: GET /tailor/garments/:id/archive produces a .hgz archive (gzip-compressed tar or zip) containing: garment_spec.json (hsk.tailor.garment_spec@1), materials/ with all referenced MAT- preset JSON rows, uv/ with atlas references resolved to embedded texture data, trims/ with all tailor_trims rows, animation/ with cached frame data if present, and a manifest.json with content hashes and format_version

POST /tailor/garments/import accepts a .hgz archive and reconstructs a draft garment in the target workspace, re-minting all IDs (GAR-, MAT-, BLK-, etc.) so the import does not collide with existing rows

An archive exported from workspace A can be imported into workspace B on a different Handshake instance and produces a garment that passes PANEL_CLOSED + SEAM_LENGTH_MATCH + FABRIC_PRESET_VALID validators on the target instance
tailor_garment_archive_export and tailor_garment_archive_import MCP tools are registered and documented in ModelManual
TailorGarmentArchiveExported and TailorGarmentArchiveImported KernelEventType variants are emitted and replayable
A garment archived before a GarmentSpec schema bump loads correctly after forward migration (reuses MT-139 migration logic)
The archive manifest.json includes format_version, source_workspace_id (anonymised), garment_id, and content_hash per asset for integrity verification on import
proof_targets: HBR-INT: EventLedger replay reconstructs archive export and import from durable state
scope.risk_if_missed: Without a portable garment archive, promoted garments cannot be shared between Handshake instances, backed up independently of the database, or handed off to collaborators; MT-317 produces a production export package (mesh + textures for Blender/UE) but not a round-trippable garment source archive that preserves the authoring data for further editing.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
